frameworks.name: natural image statistics
frameworks.one_line: co-occurrence statistics of edge elements measured in natural scenes

frameworks.name: Gestalt grouping rules
frameworks.one_line: classical perceptual organization principles tested against image statistics

synopsis.core_question: Do Gestalt grouping rules for contour integration reflect the co-occurrence statistics of edge elements in natural scenes?
key_assumptions: natural scenes contain regularities exploitable by visual system
edge elements can be characterized by position and orientation
manipulations: relative position and orientation between pairs of edge elements in natural images
key_findings: edge-element co-occurrence in natural scenes follows a geometric regularity (co-circularity)
this regularity aligns with classical Gestalt good-continuation rule
interpretations: Gestalt grouping rules may have emerged from the statistics of natural visual input
